metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4909
metadata.comment:
Rationale: ATG7 is essential for macroautophagy and in particular autophagosome formation. Authors used a voltammetry assay to measure evoked dopamine release and reuptake in slices of dorsal striatum from DAT Cre and Atg7 DAT Cre mice. They found that KO cells show a greater dopamine release and they also recover faster in a pair-pulse assay.

Importantly, due to the fact that macroautophagy relies on the subsequent activation of Atg proteins that altogether lead to the formation of autophagosomes, the phenotype described in this paper is likely not due to the lack of ATG7 itself but of basal macroautophagy.. Experimental description: The study used 8-week-old Atg7-DAT-Cre mice.

From results: "We generated dopamine neuron-specific macroautophagy-deficient mice by crossing Atg7flox/flox mice (Komatsu et al., 2005) to a line expressing Cre recombinase under the dopamine transporter (DAT) promoter (DAT Cre/+) (Zhuang et al., 2005). The progeny (Atg7flox/+;DAT Cre/+) were crossed to Atg7flox/flox to generate Atg7flox/flox;DAT Cre/+ (Atg7 DAT Cre). Because the mutant mice have a single functional copy of DAT, we used DAT Cre/+ (DAT Cre) animals as controls; these animals express two copies of wild-type Atg7 and a single functional copy of DAT."
